answers.answer: D) What the resolution order is when a user mutes a channel, an admin marks it mandatory, AND a critical alert fires simultaneously - three rules conflict with no defined priority. Reason: This defines the complete 3-way conflict resolution system. Without priority order, we can't implement ANY features correctly. Option C only covers 2-way conflict (user vs admin). Option D covers all 3 rules: user mute, admin mandatory, AND critical alert. The resolution order determines: 1) Can critical alerts bypass user mute? 2) Can admin mandatory override user mute? 3) Can critical alert override admin mandatory? This is the foundational design decision that must be resolved first.

ROOT CAUSE: DNS/Service Discovery failure in us-east-1 (started ~2:00 PM)

CAUSAL CHAIN:
2:00 PM - DNS failure (Ticket 4)
  ↓
2:10 PM - CDC stream fails (Ticket 3) - Can't resolve DB hostname
  ↓
2:15 PM - Frontend API 504s (Ticket 1) - Gateway can't resolve backend IPs
  ↓
2:20 PM - Push notifications stop (Ticket 2) - Can't resolve queue/DB endpoints
  ↓
2:30 PM - QA tests fail (Ticket 5) - External API calls timeout

EVIDENCE:
- Timeline matches: DNS first, cascading failures follow
- All symptoms network/connectivity related
- Local operations work (health checks, unit tests)
- Remote operations fail (API calls, CDC, external APIs)
- DevOps explicitly reported DNS failures

FIX: Restore DNS/service discovery in us-east-1.
